Adversarially verified gapClosed=true. Root-cause structural fix: replaced the hand-maintained DisclosureScanInput subset with collectFreeTextFields(), a depth-bounded cycle-guarded walk that scans EVERY persisted string surface (content, title, tags, all metadata free-text, author.name/id, AND tenant_id - the leaked column); only an explicit ENUM_CONSTRAINED_FIELDS allow-list is skipped, inverting the failure mode to fail-safe. New schema-coverage guard test derives the persisted free-text leaf set from the live Zod schemas and fails if any leaf is unscanned (verified red on a simulated new field). Skeptic enumerated columns from the DDL+insert() itself and drove the real insert() with 50 probes (SSN/comp/token/homoglyph across every free-text column): all rejected count==0, clean control writes. common 110, store 155, apps/api 276, typecheck 0. Adversarially verified gapClosed=true (corrected invariant: same ordered set -> same chain head; per-event order-independence is by-design, delegated to 8da.8). v2 canonical serialiser excludes wallclock (timestamp-independent entry_hash proven across 2026/2027/2029/2030); non-destructive migration keeps v1 frozen tamper-evidence; mixed v1/v2 verifies in one pass via per-row hash_version dispatch. Combined with the deterministic audit-event id fix, the real promote() path reproduces byte-identical entry_hash across clones. INTKB #206 commit 55cff98.

Govern-at-merge gate shipped + adversarially proven. Draft PR #207 (qmd-team-intent-kb, branch feat/epic1-govern-at-merge-gate, commit ca34659, NOT merged). mergeGovern() is a re-derivation-over-union pass reusing every existing primitive: union + id-dedup by content-derived UUIDv5, sort-by-id (the one new discipline = commutativity keystone), re-project each row to untrusted MemoryCandidate, assertDisclosureClean() fail-closed first, then full PolicyPipeline with accreting existingHashes seeded from getAllContentHashes(), promote survivors via canonical path with deterministic mergeClock so timestamps + audit chain re-walk identically across clones. Independent auditor (not reusing curator fixtures, called public mergeGovern entrypoint) confirmed gapClosed=true: (a) AWS-key/SSN/salary content rows AND a metadata-buried AWS key all QUARANTINED at the disclosure choke point, absent from durable curated_memories; (b) cross-clone content duplicate deduped to exactly one survivor by content-id; (c) A-union-B vs B-union-A byte-identical governed state (sha256 4ae3c0ee...) AND identical audit chain (sha256 28f0608a...), both verifyAuditChain breaks=0, byte-identity held even when the same union is partitioned across clones differently; (d) every survivor independently re-passed assertDisclosureClean + PolicyPipeline. Differential mutation testing proved tests non-vacuous (bypassing disclosure gate -> secrets rode in; admitting policy-rejected rows -> dedup probe failed), then source reverted byte-identical (sha256 match vs HEAD). Three smuggle vectors all FAILED (metadata free-text secret, content-id collision, reordering attack). 602 tests green; CI all-green (validate/gitleaks/semgrep). Working tree clean, on feature branch. EPIC 1 children 8da.7 (Ed25519 signed DAG anchor) + 8da.8 (merge-aware verifier owning canonical ordering) now build on this.

Adversarially verified gapClosed=true by an independent harness (real production modules, not the impl's own tests). Per-actor Ed25519 signed DAG merge anchor: parents[]=the two clone heads as an order-independent set, lamportClock, embedded signer public key, detached Ed25519 signature over the canonical merged head. The bare hash-chain was defeated by tamper-then-rehash (verifyAuditChain breaks:0) but the signed anchor CAUGHT it (HISTORY_REWRITTEN/DAG_HEAD_MISMATCH); a keyless forger failed (20,000 brute signatures, 0 accepted). No private key committed (ephemeral keys in tests, .gitignore blocks plaintext). 180/180 store tests. PR #208 commit c8de18e.

Adversarially verified gapClosed=true. Merge-aware verifier verifyMergeAuditChain: per-clone linear validation (ENTRY_HASH_MISMATCH on a tampered clone), verifier-owned canonical content-id ordering (canonicalMergeOrder, not gate-trusted), merged re-walk (PREV_LINK on a reordered merge), and signed DAG anchor cross-check (DAG_SIGNATURE_INVALID / DAG_PARENT_MISMATCH / DAG_HEAD_MISMATCH / DAG_ANCHOR_MISSING). 180/180 store tests, lint/typecheck clean.
